cefr.note: Estimativa baseada nas faixas oficiais CEFR do MET (Michigan Language Assessment). A conversão de acertos para scaled score é aproximada (linear).

bands.desc: Usuário proficiente — compreende com facilidade discursos e textos longos e complexos.
Usuário independente avançado — compreende ideias principais e pontos de vista em contextos sociais, profissionais e acadêmicos.
Usuário independente — compreende os pontos principais de fala e textos claros sobre assuntos familiares.
Usuário básico — compreende informações essenciais de mensagens curtas, simples e previsíveis.
Abaixo do nível mínimo medido pelo MET nesta seção.

sections.name: Listening · Part 1
sections.instructions: In this part of the test, you will hear short conversations between two people. After each conversation, you will hear a question about it. Choose the best answer to the question. There are 19 questions in Part 1. The conversations and questions will not be repeated.

sections.name: Listening · Part 2
sections.instructions: In this part of the test, you will hear longer conversations between two people. After each conversation, you will answer some questions about it. There are 14 questions in Part 2. The conversations and questions will not be repeated. If you want to, you may take notes as you listen.

sections.name: Listening · Part 3
sections.instructions: In this part, you will hear some short talks. After each talk, you will answer some questions about it. There are 17 questions in Part 3. The talks and questions will not be repeated. If you want to, you may take notes as you listen.

sections.name: Grammar
sections.instructions: This section focuses on your ability to use English grammar. Each question has only one correct answer.

sections.name: Reading
sections.instructions: This section focuses on your ability to understand written English. Read each passage and answer the questions about it.

questions.text: What does the woman say about the bus?
opts: It stops in a different place.
questions.expl: A mulher diz que o ônibus deveria ter passado "five minutes ago" e explica: "No, it's just late" — está atrasado por causa de uma obra na Main Street, não quebrado.

questions.text: What will the woman probably do the following day?
opts: give the man's family a ride
take the train to the city
questions.expl: Perguntada se vai dirigir ou pegar o trem, ela responde: "Definitely the train. Traffic and parking will be a nightmare."

questions.text: What does the woman hope to do?
questions.expl: Ela torce pelo micro-ondas novo porque anda gastando demais comprando almoço fora — a ideia é passar a trazer comida de casa e poder esquentá-la no escritório.

questions.text: What will the students do tomorrow?
opts: call the professor for their grades
make appointments to see the professor
questions.expl: O professor manda deixar os quizzes corrigidos na caixa em frente à sala: "The students will be expecting them there tomorrow" — os alunos vão retirá-los amanhã.

questions.text: What does the woman most likely want to do?
questions.expl: O vendedor sugere comprar um aparelho novo, mas ela responde que tem seguro do celular, "so I think there's a better solution than that" — ou seja, prefere consertá-lo pelo seguro.

questions.text: How did the man feel when he heard the news?
questions.expl: "My supervisor and I were both taken aback by the news" — a expressão "taken aback" significa surpreso, pego de surpresa.

questions.text: What does the woman imply about the man?
opts: He should continue down the street.
He's going in the wrong direction.
questions.expl: Que ele está na direção errada a mulher DIZ diretamente ("You are turned around") — logo, não é o que ela "implica". O que fica implícito é a consequência: com a estação ainda a quatro quarteirões dali, ele vai acabar se atrasando.

questions.text: How does the man feel?
questions.expl: As respostas hesitantes — "as ready as I'll ever be, I guess" e "We'll see about that" — mostram insegurança e nervosismo, apesar das semanas de preparação.

questions.text: What is probably true about the company?
opts: It has lost many staff recently.
questions.expl: "Our orders have tripled since then. Everyone's maxed out, and we can afford to hire" — pedidos triplicaram em seis meses: a empresa está crescendo rapidamente.

questions.text: What happened when the man went to university?
opts: He took his instrument with him.
He began playing a new instrument.
He signed up for music classes.
questions.expl: "When I went to university, I moved away and left the piano at home" — ao ir para a universidade, ele parou de tocar piano.

questions.text: What does the woman say about the article?
opts: She read part of it earlier.
She doesn't remember what it was about.
She looked it over very quickly.
She won't read it for another few days.
questions.expl: "I had to read an excerpt of it for a different class the other day" — ela já tinha lido um trecho (excerpt) do artigo antes, para outra disciplina.

questions.text: What kind of toy does the man want to buy?
questions.expl: "I'd prefer something without so many bells and whistles" — "bells and whistles" são recursos/funções extras; ele quer um brinquedo simples, como os de antigamente.

questions.text: What are the speakers discussing?
questions.expl: "Are you ready for tomorrow's test?... I'm just not very good with numbers" — a prova envolve números: é a prova de matemática.

questions.text: Why will the professor contact the class?
opts: to give them materials for homework
questions.expl: Como a livraria está sem o livro-texto, o professor decide: "I'll send the reading to the whole class" — vai mandar o material da tarefa para todos.

questions.text: What are the speakers mainly discussing?
questions.expl: "I think they'll compete for a championship... but they do have a lot of new players and a difficult schedule" — eles avaliam as chances (o potencial) do time nesta temporada.

questions.text: What does the man describe as annoying?
questions.expl: "If my flight is canceled, it might be days before I can get another one. That's happened to me before. Such a pain" — o que ele chama de irritante são os atrasos/transtornos de viagem.

questions.text: When should the man probably feel better?
questions.expl: O médico diz que o creme "won't work immediately, but in a few days it should stop itching" — alguns dias ≈ dois ou três; se não melhorar até o fim da semana, ele deve voltar.

questions.text: What are the speakers mainly talking about?
questions.expl: O ar-condicionado é só o gancho: a conversa gira em torno do calorão fora de época — "summer started six weeks early", "maybe it'll cool off in a couple of days".

questions.text: How does the man feel about the new policy?
opts: He thinks it benefits all students.
He thinks it is a good idea.
He believes it will affect him negatively.
He believes it will make little difference.
questions.expl: "Probably not the worst thing... I don't think it'll be that big a deal. Besides, it'll make campus safer" — ele acha a medida boa (mas não chega a dizer que beneficia todos os alunos).

questions.text: What are the speakers mainly discussing?
opts: the state of the man's business
a seminar the man will attend
questions.expl: Toda a conversa acontece na convenção anual de empresários: "that's why we come, to learn from each other", "coming here each year is a great way to learn..." — o assunto principal é o evento.

questions.text: Why does the man mention next year?
opts: He will start a new business then.
He will miss the conference then.
He thinks the economy will be better then.
He thinks his business may be closed then.
questions.expl: "We'll be lucky if we break even this year. The forecast is brighter for next year though" — ele menciona o ano que vem porque espera uma economia melhor.

questions.text: What is the man likely to do next?
questions.expl: Antes do seminário, ele diz: "I need to check in on things back in my office before that" — o próximo passo é contatar (ligar para) o escritório.

questions.text: What does the man mean when he says:
opts: His business isn't yet ready to open.
His business may not make money.
questions.expl: "Break even" = empatar receitas e despesas, sem lucro nem prejuízo. "We'll be lucky if we break even" = teremos sorte se ao menos empatarmos — o negócio talvez não dê dinheiro.

questions.text: Why does the man tell the woman she's lucky?
opts: The item she wants is on sale.
The store is open late today.
He has what she is looking for.
questions.expl: "You're very lucky. The one in the window is actually our last, and I believe it's your size" — ele tem exatamente o que ela procura, no tamanho dela.

questions.text: What problem does the woman have?
opts: The store doesn't have her size.
Someone else bought what she wanted.
She doesn't like anything in the store.
questions.expl: "I left my credit card at home, and I don't think I have enough cash" — ela não tem dinheiro suficiente no momento.

questions.text: What does the man mean when he says:
opts: He will close the store early.
He will sell the suit to someone else.
He will reserve the suit temporarily.
questions.expl: "I'll make an exception and set it aside so that no one else can buy it" — abrir uma exceção e separar a peça até a loja fechar é reservá-la temporariamente.

questions.text: What are the speakers discussing?
opts: a course textbook the woman is returning
a course textbook the woman needs
a book the woman had reserved online
a book the woman's friend recommended
questions.expl: "A friend of mine was in here earlier today and saw a book that might help me with a research project" — é um livro que a amiga viu e recomendou.

questions.text: Why does the man ask about the woman's research?
opts: to send her to the correct building
to suggest a topic for a project
to help her find what she needs
to learn more about her interests
questions.expl: "Tell me more about your research. Maybe that will point us in the right direction" — ele pergunta para conseguir localizar o livro que ela precisa.

questions.text: What will the woman probably do?
opts: get the electronic version of the book
borrow the book from her friend
check the book out from the library
come back later to get the book
questions.expl: Ao saber do empréstimo de dois dias, ela conclui: "I won't need it for more than two days, so I'll just use the hard copy" — vai retirar o exemplar físico da biblioteca.

questions.text: What does the woman mean when she says:
opts: The book didn't contain useful information.
She didn't know what to do.
She didn't find the name of the book.
questions.expl: "I came up empty" = voltei de mãos vazias; ela pesquisou on-line "to see if I could figure out what it was called" e não conseguiu descobrir o nome do livro.

questions.text: What did the woman do the previous semester?
opts: started to use technology while teaching
trained her assistant to teach her course
asked students about how they used laptops
gathered evidence about students' use of laptops
questions.expl: "My teaching assistant and I gathered informal data this past semester... up to 70% of my students are doing something on their computers other than taking notes" — ela coletou evidências sobre o uso dos laptops.

questions.text: Why does the woman talk about research studies?
opts: to explain how she got her idea
to argue against the man's opinion
to describe the type of work she enjoys
to explain what she'll discuss with her students
questions.expl: "I'm going to lay out the rationale... go over the studies that show that taking notes by hand leads to better retention" — os estudos são o que ela pretende apresentar aos alunos para justificar a nova regra.

questions.text: What does the man think about the woman's idea?
opts: It is better than his idea.
It is already accepted at their school.
It might be rejected by the administration.
questions.expl: "I might even give it a try in my classes, and who knows? Maybe you'll start a school-wide trend" — ele acha que a ideia pode influenciar outros professores.

questions.text: What is the talk mainly about?
opts: the different types of birds in a park
the eating habits of a type of bird
the speaker's research on endangered species
the characteristics of a type of bird
questions.expl: O guia descreve o blue heron por completo — ninho, tamanho ("well over a meter tall"), aparência e hábitos — ou seja, as características de uma espécie de ave.

questions.text: What does the speaker suggest the listeners do?
opts: use equipment to help them see better
use equipment to climb a tree
try to take a photo of the bird
questions.expl: "If you don't have binoculars, we have a telescope set up over here that you can use" — equipamentos para enxergar melhor.

questions.text: What does the speaker think the bird standing on the nest is doing?
questions.expl: "It's probably standing guard over the eggs, watching for owls and hawks" — a ave no ninho está protegendo os ovos.

questions.text: Why does the speaker mention fish?
opts: to tell the listeners what they'll do next
to compare the behavior of different animals
to explain what one bird is doing
to describe what lives in the lake
questions.expl: O parceiro está na água rasa "probably looking for fish, which blue herons like to eat" — os peixes explicam o que aquela ave está fazendo (caçando comida).

questions.text: What is the speaker mainly discussing?
questions.expl: O arquiteto apresenta "my firm's ideas for the design of your company's new supermarket" — os planos do novo prédio/loja do cliente.

questions.text: Why does the speaker mention a home appliance store?
opts: to suggest an interesting storage method
to criticize a common sales strategy
to compare different types of shoppers
questions.expl: A loja de eletrodomésticos exemplifica o layout de corredores compridos que "keeps customers in the store as long as possible" — uma estratégia de vendas comum que ele critica por frustrar o cliente.

questions.text: What will the meeting participants most likely do next?
questions.expl: "Now, let's look at some sketches that illustrate what we have in mind" — o próximo passo é ver os desenhos do projeto.

questions.text: What does the speaker suggest about his team when he says:
opts: They had difficulty making a decision.
They were confused about the instructions.
They did not want to change their minds.
They had concerns about an idea.
questions.expl: "On the fence" = em cima do muro, indeciso. "At first we were on the fence about which one to go with" — a equipe teve dificuldade para decidir.

questions.text: What is the talk mainly about?
opts: student activities at a high school
academic programs at a high school
teaching staff at a high school
an upcoming sporting event at a high school
questions.expl: A fala apresenta "the various sports and other extracurricular activities" da escola — as atividades estudantis.

questions.text: What does the speaker say about other teachers?
opts: They help with the sports programs.
They teach a variety of subjects.
They will also talk to the parents.
They will take the parents on a tour.
questions.expl: "Then you'll hear from some other teachers who also lead programs" — os outros professores também vão falar aos pais (Fine Arts, organizações acadêmicas, clubes sociais).

questions.text: Why does the speaker mention some equipment?
opts: to explain what parents will need to buy
to explain how students play sports safely
to explain a benefit of two sports teams
to explain a disadvantage of a sports team
questions.expl: "We also have soccer and tennis teams, both of which received brand new equipment last year" — o equipamento novo é citado como um benefício desses dois times.

questions.text: What does the speaker say about participating in a sport?
opts: It is required at this school.
It is not appropriate for all students.
questions.expl: "Participating in a sport is a great character building experience, a way to develop teamwork and leadership skills" — uma experiência valiosa.

questions.text: What will the audience probably do next?
questions.expl: A fala termina com "now let's hear from Mr. Morris about Fine Arts" — o diretor de artes falará sobre música, arte e teatro.

questions.text: What will the students be doing?
opts: selling some of the pottery they make
questions.expl: "They'll try to recreate these ancient objects" / "how will these replicas be created?" — os alunos farão réplicas (cópias) de objetos antigos.

questions.text: What does the speaker wish he could do?
opts: be a student at City College
questions.expl: "[I] wondered what it would be like to hold them in my hands" — desde criança o locutor queria poder tocar as peças do museu.

questions.text: What is mentioned about the oven?
opts: It was made for this project.
It was designed by the students.
questions.expl: "The bowls will be fired in a wood oven built especially for this project" — o forno foi construído especialmente para o projeto.

questions.text: What will happen in May at the museum?
opts: There will be an exhibit of student pottery.
Students will teach a pottery class.
The museum will be temporarily closed.
Art scholars will give lectures about Greek pottery.
questions.expl: "To celebrate the project's conclusion in May, the museum plans to hold an exhibit of the students' reproductions" — haverá uma exposição da cerâmica dos alunos.

questions.text: Next month, you _____ in this department.
questions.expl: "Next month" mostra que a frase fala do futuro: "you will start working" — "você vai começar a trabalhar".

questions.text: _____ chemistry that we must study today, but also physics.
questions.expl: A ideia é "não é só química que temos que estudar hoje, mas também física". Em inglês: "It is not only chemistry... but also physics."

questions.text: Artists _____ will show their work at the festival next week.
questions.expl: "Of all kinds" = "de todos os tipos": artistas de todos os tipos vão mostrar seu trabalho no festival.

questions.text: The natural history museum _____ most popular attraction in the area.
questions.expl: O museu "continua sendo a atração mais popular" = "has remained the most popular attraction". Antes de "most popular", o inglês pede "the".

questions.text: Ms. Simpson was _____ in the neighborhood improvement project.
questions.expl: "Very much involved" = "muito envolvida" — é assim que dizemos que alguém participou bastante de algo.

questions.text: It is required that students' grades _____ in digital format, rather than on paper.
questions.expl: Depois de "It is required that..." ("é obrigatório que..."), o verbo fica na forma básica: "be submitted" — "que as notas sejam enviadas".

questions.text: _____ the market analysts nor the public know if stocks will recover next year.
questions.expl: Para dizer "nem os analistas nem o público", o inglês usa o par "neither... nor".

questions.text: Lisa put _____ pressure on herself to succeed in her classes.
questions.expl: "Pressure" (pressão) não dá para contar em unidades, então dizemos "a tremendous amount of pressure" — "uma quantidade enorme de pressão". Repare que a expressão completa precisa do "a" e do "of".

questions.text: As _____ in this report, our company's future looks strong.
questions.expl: "As pointed out in this report" = "como apontado neste relatório" — o jeito comum de citar de onde veio a informação.

questions.text: When Rebecca first moved to Atlanta, she knew almost no one there _____ than Susan.
questions.expl: "Other than Susan" = "além da Susan / fora a Susan": ela não conhecia quase ninguém além da Susan.

questions.text: To see who had finished the reading, the teacher asked for _____.
questions.expl: "A show of hands" é a expressão para "votação levantando as mãos" — o professor pediu que levantassem a mão para ver quem tinha terminado.

questions.text: There is an entire engineering field dedicated to workplace _____.
questions.expl: Aqui precisamos do nome da qualidade: "efficiency" (eficiência) — "um campo dedicado à eficiência no trabalho". "Efficient" e "efficiently" descreveriam algo, não nomeiam a qualidade.

questions.text: Jeff couldn't decide whether _____ to go out with friends.
questions.expl: "Whether to stay home or go out" = "se ficava em casa ou saía" — as duas alternativas aparecem com "to + verbo".

questions.text: _____ for a scholarship this year?
questions.expl: Pergunta sobre algo em andamento: "Is she applying for a scholarship this year?" = "Ela está se candidatando a uma bolsa este ano?". ("Apply herself" mudaria o sentido para "esforçar-se".)

questions.text: My brother _____ extra income from his new part-time job.
questions.expl: "Bring in" é o verbo para "trazer/gerar dinheiro": "is bringing in extra income" — "está ganhando uma renda extra".

questions.text: Roger said he _____ to the party at Sally's place this coming Saturday.
questions.expl: Roger contou um plano: "Roger said he was going to the party" — "disse que ia à festa". Depois de "said", o verbo recua para o passado.

questions.text: The other day, I heard an interesting _____ on the radio.
questions.expl: Em inglês não se diz "a news"; a unidade é "a piece of news" — "uma notícia interessante".

questions.text: Help yourself _____ of the soup in the kitchen.
questions.expl: "Help yourself to..." = "sirva-se de...": "Help yourself to some of the soup" — "sirva-se da sopa".

questions.text: Generally speaking, the more funding a university receives _____ its facilities will be.
questions.expl: Estrutura "quanto mais..., melhor": "the more funding a university receives, the better its facilities will be".

questions.text: _____ raining, we would have visited the zoo.
questions.expl: "Had it not been raining" é o jeito mais formal de dizer "If it had not been raining" — "se não estivesse chovendo, teríamos visitado o zoológico".

units.title: Passagem 1 · Questões 71–75
units.passageIntro: This passage is about a new type of health care service.
units.passage:
What if the next time you need to visit the doctor, you could just turn on your computer instead of driving to the doctor's office for your appointment? Right before your appointment, you go online to complete a questionnaire about your medical needs, and then, when you are ready, you speak to a medical professional by phone or video chat. The doctor writes up a plan for your treatment and sends it to you by email. A prescription for medication, if needed, goes directly to the pharmacy of your choice. If your pharmacy provides a home delivery service, you may never need to leave your own home during your illness.

This idea of telemedicine has been gaining popularity in the United States over the last several years as it offers benefits to both consumers and health care providers. For people who live in isolated, rural areas, a trip to the nearest doctor may take hours, so telemedicine is not only convenient, but perhaps the only reasonably available medical care in their community. Even for those who have ready access to medical services, the availability of online consultations may lead patients to seek medical help earlier and more frequently, instead of waiting and eventually needing emergency care, which is much more expensive to both patients and insurance companies.

Of course, there are limits to virtual medicine. Many diagnoses require lab tests or X-rays that cannot be done remotely. More than that, many doctors feel that face-to-face communication with their patients is essential. During online consultations, doctors might easily miss subtle signals that would help them better understand their patients' problems. Even so, virtual medicine is likely to become more prevalent in the medical care of tomorrow, as patients continue to look for easy access and convenience, and insurers identify ways to reduce their expenses.

questions.text: Why does the passage begin with a question?
opts: to show how little is known about telemedicine
to suggest the possibility of telemedicine
to question the effectiveness of telemedicine
to criticize the current research on telemedicine
questions.expl: A pergunta inicial ("What if... you could just turn on your computer instead of driving to the doctor's office?") convida o leitor a imaginar a telemedicina — apresenta a possibilidade, sem criticar nem questionar.

questions.text: According to the passage, why could pharmacy delivery service be important?
opts: It lets people stay at home when sick.
It is helpful for people who are very ill.
Pharmacy employees can check on patients.
Patients can get their medications faster.
questions.expl: O texto diz que, se a farmácia entrega em casa, "you may never need to leave your own home during your illness" — a pessoa doente pode ficar em casa.

questions.text: In the third sentence of paragraph 2, which word is closest in meaning to ready?
questions.expl: "Ready access" = acesso rápido, imediato. Entre as opções, "quick" é a mais próxima.

questions.text: What claim does the author make to support the belief that telemedicine can reduce costs?
opts: Doctors do not need to pay for office space.
Fewer lab tests will be conducted.
Patients will need less emergency care.
questions.expl: O texto afirma que as consultas on-line levam o paciente a procurar ajuda mais cedo, "instead of waiting and eventually needing emergency care, which is much more expensive" — menos atendimentos de emergência = menos custo.

questions.text: In the third sentence of paragraph 3, what does the word that refer to?
opts: the importance of face-to-face communication with patients
the inability to perform certain tests remotely
the fact that some doctors dislike telemedicine
questions.expl: Em "More than that...", o "that" retoma o que acabou de ser dito: muitos diagnósticos exigem exames (lab tests/X-rays) que não podem ser feitos a distância.

units.title: Passagem 2 · Questões 76–80
units.passageIntro: This passage is about changes in language.
units.passage:
It is commonly accepted that languages change over time, with new words added and older terms dropped from common use. Charles Darwin, more famously associated with biological evolution, claimed that languages were also evolving over time, but this was only a speculative account of linguistic evolution. Modern research now suggests that Darwin's assertion was right and that words used most commonly in everyday language evolve at the slowest rate, while rarer words are more likely to change.

Researchers in the United Kingdom investigated the evolution of language by studying cognates, words that sound similar in different languages and express the same meaning, to identify how much variation now exists in Indo-European languages after 6,000 to 10,000 years of evolution. They discovered that frequently used words are more likely to have cognates, while less common words are more likely to be unrelated in different languages. The findings allowed a "mutation rate" to be calculated, based on frequency of use, which showed that the most common vocabulary exhibited almost no variation in 10,000 years. Vocabulary used less often was replaced in as few as 750 years.

A second study, in the United States, examined the roots of English from over 1,200 years ago to compile a list of 177 irregular old English verbs. Researchers discovered that of the original irregular verbs only 98 remain irregular today, suggesting that verbs tend to become "regularized" over time. The rarest verbs in the list were the most likely to have been regularized. A statistical analysis of these verbs indicated that if one was used 100 times less frequently than another, it would evolve 10 times faster than the more commonly used verb. Both studies support the notion that languages evolve much like natural species do.

questions.text: According to the passage, why was Darwin's theory of linguistic evolution unproven?
opts: He did not consider Indo-European languages.
His theory did not account for how verbs evolved.
His theory was not based on experimental evidence.
questions.expl: O texto diz que a afirmação de Darwin "was only a speculative account of linguistic evolution" — era especulativa, sem base em evidência experimental.

questions.text: How did researchers in the United Kingdom study linguistic evolution?
opts: by identifying the most important words in Indo-European languages
by determining how long it has taken Indo-European languages to evolve
by comparing cognates found in Indo-European languages
by examining how different European languages define cognates
questions.expl: "Researchers in the United Kingdom investigated the evolution of language by studying cognates... in Indo-European languages" — comparando cognatos dessas línguas.

questions.text: What did the "mutation rate" allow researchers to understand?
opts: that rarely used words took 10,000 years to change
whether or not a word was related to other words
how much variation occurred in words of certain frequencies
if a word was evolving quickly enough
questions.expl: A "mutation rate", calculada "based on frequency of use", mostrou quanta variação sofreram as palavras conforme sua frequência (as comuns quase não mudaram; as raras mudaram em até 750 anos).

questions.text: Which irregular verbs are most likely to become regularized?
opts: those that are the least irregular
those that are most commonly used
those that have cognates in other languages
questions.expl: "The rarest verbs in the list were the most likely to have been regularized" — os verbos usados com menos frequência são os que mais se regularizam.

questions.text: What do the two studies reported in the passage have in common?
opts: Both investigate the relationship between word frequency and rate of change.
Both identify the most commonly used words in a variety of European languages.
Both challenge Charles Darwin's theory that languages will evolve over time.
Both analyze languages to determine the reasons for important words being replaced.
questions.expl: Os dois estudos relacionam frequência de uso com velocidade de mudança: cognatos por frequência no primeiro; no segundo, o verbo 100× menos usado evolui 10× mais rápido.

units.title: Textos A, B e C · Questões 81–90

texts.html:
Midvillage's Fifth Annual International Film Festival

April 1–8

Come check out our film festival. It's bigger and better than ever!

  • Over 50 films representing 25 countries
  • A wide selection of award-winning entries — dramas, comedies, documentaries, and animation
  • Screenings featuring introductions and panel discussions with the films' stars
  • Post-festival events including workshops with leading producers, directors, screenwriters, and actors

Reserve your tickets early at midvillagefilmfest.com or by calling 1-800-555-0000. Films will be shown at 10 different theaters around Midvillage at conveniently scheduled afternoon and evening times.

Midvillage's Daily News staff reporter Gary Davis interviews film critic Sophie Allen.

Q: In your opinion, who was the most influential film director of the 20th century?

A: Oh, without a doubt, Federico Fellini! He was an award-winning, highly respected Italian filmmaker who lived from 1920–1993.

Q: Tell us about his early life.

A: Fellini was born in the small town of Rimini, Italy. In 1926, he saw his first film. It had a strong effect on him and sparked his lifelong love of cinema.

Q: How did he start his career in the film business?

A: He went to Rome in 1939 and began writing newspaper and magazine articles. This put him in contact with screenwriters and he soon received his first screenwriting credit. In 1942, Fellini went to Africa, where he transitioned from writing to being a filmmaker.

Q: What was his most significant contribution to the world of cinema?

A: I would say that it was his unique, very personal style, which combined memory, dreams, and fantasy. Fellini's approach can be seen in many of today's most renowned films.

Value of Using Movies as a Teaching Tool

by Dr. Hannah Peters

Using film in the classroom has a positive effect on both young children and adolescent learners.

Movies, when used appropriately, can be an effective teaching tool for children. This is because, in addition to being attention-grabbing, they have the potential to teach life skills and promote values needed in today's world. The vivid images evoked by film bring about strong emotions and can spark a child's interest and imagination. By providing a picture of people, places, and moral dilemmas, many of which may be new to children, films raise awareness of social issues and promote social responsibility. In turn, children become introspective and reflect on their own lives, seeing them more clearly and, perhaps, differently. They learn to think more deeply and critically about the world, and develop feelings of compassion for others. Thus, their lives are enriched.

Movies are very much a part of today's world, and older students have become quite used to this fact. When film is brought into the classroom, these students can connect with concepts in ways that are comfortable and familiar to them. Movie topics are generally current and relevant, and therefore more likely to generate interest and hold students' attention. By engaging with the visuals, students can learn and retain more.

As an added bonus to teachers, those who use film in class are generally seen as friendlier and more human, generating a stronger teacher-student relationship. As students feel more comfortable with the media and the instructor, they learn and remember more.

Dr. Hannah Peters is a movie producer and a professor of film studies at Midvillage College.

questions.text: What can participants do after the festival?
opts: watch a special screening of foreign films
attend sessions with professionals in the field
try out for a part in a movie
questions.expl: O anúncio lista "post-festival events including workshops with leading producers, directors, screenwriters, and actors" — sessões com profissionais da área depois do festival.

questions.text: In the first sentence of the final paragraph, what does Reserve mean?
questions.expl: "Reserve your tickets early" = garanta/assegure seus ingressos com antecedência — "secure".

questions.text: What is the main purpose of section B?
opts: to explain the work of a film critic
to tell about a movie director
questions.expl: A entrevista inteira é sobre Federico Fellini — vida, carreira e contribuição ao cinema. O propósito é falar de um diretor.

questions.text: In the first question, what does influential mean?
opts: having the power to cause change
questions.expl: "Influential" = influente, com poder de provocar mudança; a crítica reforça isso ao dizer que a abordagem de Fellini "can be seen in many of today's most renowned films".

questions.text: How did Fellini begin his career?
opts: as a travel writer in Africa
questions.expl: "He went to Rome in 1939 and began writing newspaper and magazine articles" — ele começou como jornalista.

questions.text: In the last answer, why does Sophie Allen mention dreams?
opts: to talk about a popular movie topic
to tell about her hopes for the future
to describe a method of an important director
questions.expl: Os sonhos aparecem na descrição do estilo de Fellini — "his unique, very personal style, which combined memory, dreams, and fantasy" — o método de um diretor importante.

questions.text: In the second paragraph, why does the author mention imagination?
opts: to state something that can be positively affected by films
to describe something that parents try to encourage
to refer to something that is harmed by watching movies
to illustrate how teachers must plan lessons using films
questions.expl: "The vivid images evoked by film... can spark a child's interest and imagination" — a imaginação é algo que os filmes afetam positivamente.

questions.text: According to the author, what is one reason older children can connect to film?
opts: They are used to its presence in their lives.
They spend a lot of time playing video games.
They often need to watch movies for homework.
questions.expl: "Movies are very much a part of today's world, and older students have become quite used to this fact" — eles estão acostumados à presença do cinema em suas vidas.

questions.text: In the second sentence of paragraph 4, what does they refer to?
questions.expl: "As students feel more comfortable with the media and the instructor, they learn and remember more" — "they" retoma "students".

questions.text: Who is most likely to lead a workshop after the Midvillage Film Festival?
questions.expl: O festival terá workshops com "leading producers" (texto A), e a Dra. Hannah Peters é "a movie producer" (texto C) — a única produtora entre as opções.

units.title: Textos A, B e C · Questões 91–100

texts.html:
Mayday Inn: Come to beautiful Victorian Cape May!

Founded in 1857, Cape May is one of the oldest ocean resorts in the United States. Our graceful hotel has spacious rooms with old-world charm and modern conveniences, like antique furniture and Internet access. Situated on a tree-lined street in the historic district, we are surrounded by fine examples of Victorian architecture.

Our friendly staff will happily help you:

  • book a ride in a horse-drawn carriage
  • take a city tour
  • visit the lighthouse
  • arrange visits to antique stores

Visit us online at www.maydayinn.com.

History 287/English Literature 242

The Victorian Era: An Overview
Professor Donald Wilson

The United Kingdom's Queen Victoria reigned from 1837 until 1901. Characterized by a rapidly growing middle class, this crucial period saw dramatic changes to British society.

Through lectures, discussions, and readings, learn about the Victorian era by exploring three time periods:

Early (1830–1848)
Britain's first railroad opened, allowing the middle class to travel, resulting in the development of a tourist infrastructure.

Middle (1848–1870)
The Great Exhibition of 1851, the first world's fair, presented views of modern architecture. Private homes went from being part of the workplace to separate, self-contained structures.

Late (1870–1901)
This period saw the beginning of the decline of Britain as a world power.

The second half of the course will focus on the publishing industry of the Victorian era. With the increase in literacy, the public's appetite for books and magazines grew. Novels and works of nonfiction were presented to the public as a series, in monthly parts over a period of time. Several such selections will be included on the course reading list.

Charles Dickens: His Life and Work

by Donald Wilson

Charles Dickens (1812–1870) was a renowned British writer whose many Victorian era works are considered literary classics.

The second of eight children, Dickens was born into poverty. Due to unfortunate circumstances, he spent a good portion of his youth working to help support his family. His loneliness and feelings of abandonment and betrayal by the adults who were supposed to take care of him became common themes in his work.

At age 15, for the second time in his childhood, he was forced to leave school and contribute to his family's income. He began an office job that became the starting point of his career as an author. Dickens was later employed as a reporter, then a magazine publisher.

One of his early novels, Oliver Twist, was based on his experiences as a child, supporting himself and figuring out life on his own. The book was published chapter by chapter, and readers eagerly looked forward to the next monthly installment. Dickens's success continued with David Copperfield, A Tale of Two Cities, and Great Expectations.

Charles Dickens's large body of work went beyond the novel to include autobiography, travel books, and plays. In 1851, he even took part in a theater production that was performed before Queen Victoria.

Sadly, he was involved in a railroad accident in 1865 and suffered injuries from which he never fully recovered. Fortunately, Dickens's lengthy stories, with their unforgettable characters, live on.

Donald Wilson is a biographer of Charles Dickens and an expert on Victorian life and literature.

questions.text: In the third sentence of the paragraph, what does architecture mean?
questions.expl: "Architecture" ali se refere ao estilo/projeto das construções — o hotel é cercado por "fine examples of Victorian architecture" (belos exemplos de design de edifícios).

questions.text: What is the main purpose of section B?
opts: to tell about the life of Queen Victoria
to give details about British society
questions.expl: O texto B é a ementa de um curso universitário (History 287/English Literature 242) do professor Donald Wilson — descreve uma disciplina.

questions.text: According to the passage, what important event took place during the early Victorian era?
opts: A new form of transportation was developed.
Britain started to become less important.
questions.expl: No período inicial, "Britain's first railroad opened" — a ferrovia, uma nova forma de transporte.

questions.text: Why does the author mention private homes in the middle Victorian period?
opts: to describe housing prices of the Victorian era
to tell about a new British industry
to report on changes in family structure
to give an example of how British society changed
questions.expl: As casas que deixaram de ser parte do local de trabalho para virar estruturas separadas ilustram as "dramatic changes to British society" — um exemplo de mudança social.

questions.text: In the second sentence of the last paragraph, what does literacy mean?
questions.expl: "With the increase in literacy, the public's appetite for books and magazines grew" — "literacy" = alfabetização, capacidade de leitura.

questions.text: According to the passage, what did Dickens do for much of his youth?
questions.expl: "He spent a good portion of his youth working to help support his family" — passou boa parte da juventude trabalhando para ganhar dinheiro.

questions.text: How did the young Dickens feel about the adults in his life?
opts: They took good care of him.
questions.expl: O texto fala dos "feelings of abandonment and betrayal by the adults who were supposed to take care of him" — os adultos o decepcionaram.

questions.text: In the last sentence of paragraph 4, why does the author mention David Copperfield?
opts: to describe Dickens's most famous novel
to give an example of another Dickens novel
to name a magazine that Dickens published
to tell about an unsuccessful Dickens work
questions.expl: David Copperfield aparece na frase "Dickens's success continued with David Copperfield, A Tale of Two Cities..." — é citado como exemplo de outro romance dele.

questions.text: What was Dickens's relationship to Queen Victoria?
opts: He wrote a book for her.
He acted in a play for her.
questions.expl: "In 1851, he even took part in a theater production that was performed before Queen Victoria" — ele atuou numa peça apresentada para a rainha.

questions.text: How are Dickens's novels typical of Victorian literature?
opts: They have children as the main characters.
They often used architecture for their subject matter.
They were published in sections over time.
They tried to help those who lived in poverty.
questions.expl: O texto B diz que os romances vitorianos eram publicados "as a series, in monthly parts", e o C conta que Oliver Twist saiu "chapter by chapter" — publicação em partes ao longo do tempo.
